title: Idempotent Consumers, Inbox Records, and External Effects · Type-System Trace: Idempotency Fingerprint
summary: A consumer uses an in-memory set, marks messages complete before effects, reuses IDs across tenants, and assumes an idempotency key makes non-transactional email and payment calls exactly once. Planned artifact: Add and verify the idempotency fingerprint increment in durable inbox and duplicate-safe effect protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Reject reuse of an operation identity with a different payload fingerprint or semantic intent and retain conflict evidence.
Record inbox identity, input fingerprint, state transition, domain effect, and completion atomically in one database transaction where possible.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Idempotency Fingerprint · Theory Idempotency Fingerprint
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 3716 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reject fingerprint conflicts, suppress completed duplicates, and preserve external ambiguity; retain duplicate-safe identity through every ambiguous handoff; then make the broker confirmation disappear after the message may have committed. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
